essays:
$$\text{Matrix Multiplication:}$$
$$\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{bmatrix} \times \begin{bmatrix} 5 & 6 \\ 7 & 8 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 19 & 22 \\ 43 & 50 \end{bmatrix}$$
$$\text{To get each value you take a row from the first matrix and a column}$$
$$\text{from the second and multiply and add them up, like } 1*5+2*7 \text{ for the}$$
$$\text{top left.}$$

$$\text{CONVOLUSION (FILTER APPLIED TO IMMAGE):}$$
$$\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 0 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} 4 & 5 \end{bmatrix}$$
$$\begin{bmatrix} 0 & -1 \end{bmatrix} * \begin{bmatrix} 6 & 7 \end{bmatrix} = -3$$
$$\text{You put the filter on top of the immage patch, multiply each pair of}$$
$$\text{numbers that line up, then add all of them together to get one number.}$$

```latex
\begin{verbatim}
DEF MULTIPLICATION_TABLE(N):
    TABEL = []
    FOR I IN RANGE(1, N + 1):
        ROW = []
        FOR J IN RANGE(1, N + 1):
            ROW.APPEND(I * J)
\end{verbatim}
```
